name: Solon/Chagrin Falls
description_short: The Solon to Chagrin Falls Trail is a public recreational infrastructure project by Cleveland Metroparks, not a financial services company. This is a miscategorized entry.
description_long: The Solon to Chagrin Falls Trail is a 2-mile public recreational trail project developed by Cleveland Metroparks in partnership with the City of Solon, Ohio. This is a parks and recreation infrastructure initiative, not a consumer finance company. The project was funded through the Ohio Department of Natural Resources Clean Ohio Trails Fund and state capital programs, with approximately $1.2 million in grants plus City of Solon funding. Phase 1, which runs from SOM Center Road to the Deer Run cul-de-sac near the Solon/Bentleyville boundary, was substantially completed in October 2025 and is open to the public. The trail follows the former Wheeling and Lake Erie Railroad Company corridor and includes an enhanced bicycle and pedestrian crossing at Liberty Road. Phase 2, scheduled to begin construction in 2026, will connect the existing trail at Chagrin River Road and complete the overall project using land already owned by Cleveland Metroparks. The trail is owned by the City of Solon and managed operationally by Cleveland Metroparks. This entry appears in a consumer finance database in error, as it provides no financial services, lending, credit repair, or consumer finance assistance of any kind. The project is a community amenity providing recreational access and active transportation infrastructure.
